pi_items.source: Task description explicitly states: 'Organize all bookmarks into a new Edge Favorites folder named exactly "Labor Market Data"'
Task description explicitly states: 'Save a policy briefing to /home/ga/Desktop/labor_briefing.txt'

Web verification: All four domains are official US government domains. BLS.gov (Bureau of Labor Statistics), fred.stlouisfed.org (Federal Reserve Economic Data), census.gov (US Census Bureau), data.bls.gov (BLS data portal) are all legitimate authoritative sources

Task description requires: '(1) the seasonally adjusted US unemployment rate, (2) the month-over-month change in total nonfarm payroll employment, and (3) the US civilian labor force participation rate'

pi_items.metadata_value: Economist / Survey Researcher - compiling authoritative government data for policy analysis
pi_items.verified_value: Economist preparing a labor market briefing for a policy meeting
pi_items.source: Task description states: 'You are an economist preparing a labor market briefing for a policy meeting'
FRED series PAYEMS (https://fred.stlouisfed.org/series/PAYEMS) updated March 6, 2026. Source: BLS Current Employment Statistics (CES0000000001)
FRED series CIVPART (https://fred.stlouisfed.org/series/CIVPART) updated March 6, 2026. Source: BLS Current Population Survey

privileged_info_summary: The task requires collection of three US labor market indicators from official government sources (BLS.gov and FRED). The most recent verified data (February 2026, seasonally adjusted) shows: unemployment rate of 4.4%, nonfarm payroll employment of 158,466 thousand persons, and civilian labor force participation rate of 62.0%. All metadata claims about domains, indicators, folder name, and briefing path are accurate and align with the authoritative government sources (Bureau of Labor Statistics and Federal Reserve Economic Data database). The data is real, not synthetic, coming from official BLS surveys (Current Population Survey and Current Employment Statistics).
